name: Nava a La Pola Siera — circular via the river path of the Nora river
name_original: Nava a La Pola Siera — circular por la senda fluvial del río Nora
region_intro: Touring cycling around Nava offers diverse landscapes, from river valleys to challenging ascents. The region features routes that traverse riverside paths, such as those along the Nora river, and include significant climbs like the Les Praeres Ascent. Cyclists can experience a mix of terrain, with options ranging from moderate paths to more difficult routes through the Asturian countryside. Best touring cycling routes around Nava The most popular touring cycling route is Nava a La Pola Siera — circular via the river path of the Nora river, a 25.9 miles (41.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Nora river path, offering a scenic circular journey. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Nava a Villaviciosa — circular route, a difficult 38.4 miles (61.9 km) path. This route features substantial elevation gain, connecting Nava with Villaviciosa through varied terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Pola de Siero Dog Park – Río Nora Riverside Trail loop from Nava, a 27.3 miles (43.9 km) trail leading through the Río Nora Riverside, often completed in about 2 hours 39 minutes. Touring cycling around Nava is defined by river valleys, rolling hills, and challenging ascents. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate riverside trails to difficult routes with significant climbs. The routes in Nava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 326 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Nava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Nava?
A: Nava offers a good selection of touring cycling routes, with over 20 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from moderate riverside paths to more challenging ascents through the Asturian countryside.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Nava?
A: The terrain around Nava is quite varied, featuring river valleys, rolling hills, and challenging ascents. You'll find routes that traverse riverside paths, such as those along the Nora river, and others that include significant climbs like the Les Praeres Ascent. Many routes feature mostly paved surfaces, but some may include gravel sections.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Nava?
A: Yes, there are family-friendly options available. Routes along river paths, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029991339/nava-a-la-pola-siera-circular-via-the-river-path-of-the-nora-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nava a La Pola Siera — circular via the river path of the Nora river&lt;/a&gt;, are generally more moderate and suitable for families. These paths offer a scenic and less strenuous cycling experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Nava?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on all cycling routes can vary, many natural areas and riverside trails are generally dog-friendly. A popular option that explicitly mentions dogs is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20501761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pola de Siero Dog Park – Río Nora Riverside Trail loop from Nava&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Río Nora Riverside. Always ensure your dog is on a leash and you clean up after them.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features to see along the cycling routes in Nava?
A: The region around Nava is rich in both natural beauty and historical sites. Along your rides, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5570790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuensanta Natural Mineral Spring&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4694980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5570810" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pendón River Gorges&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1132118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Valdediós&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1132126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Recuna Medieval Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The area also boasts the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5599992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Praeres – Sierra de Peñamayor&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes starting from Nava?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Nava are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029991339/nava-a-la-pola-siera-circular-via-the-river-path-of-the-nora-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nava a La Pola Siera — circular via the river path of the Nora river&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029991237/nava-a-villaviciosa-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nava a Villaviciosa — circular route&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Nava?
A: The Ligurian Alps region, where Nava is located, offers a refreshing alpine climate, especially during the summer months. This makes summer an excellent time for touring cycling, allowing you to enjoy the vibrant lavender fields that bloom in July. Spring and early autumn also offer pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Nava?
A: The touring cycling routes around Nava are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, which include river valleys, rolling hills, and challenging ascents, as well as the variety of routes catering to different ability levels.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Nava offers several difficult routes with substantial elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029991237/nava-a-villaviciosa-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nava a Villaviciosa — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a difficult 61.9 km path with significant climbing. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31494718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Praeres Ascent from Nava – Les Praeres via Gravel Track loop from Nava&lt;/a&gt;, which includes a notable ascent.

Q: How long do touring cycling routes in Nava typically take to complete?
A: The duration of routes varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. Moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029991339/nava-a-la-pola-siera-circular-via-the-river-path-of-the-nora-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nava a La Pola Siera — circular via the river path of the Nora river&lt;/a&gt; can take around 2 hours 48 minutes. More difficult and longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029991237/nava-a-villaviciosa-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nava a Villaviciosa — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, may require over 4 hours to complete.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink along the touring cycling routes in Nava?
A: While specific cafes and pubs directly on every route are not detailed, the region is known for its local culture and products. You'll find small shops selling regional delicacies like lavender products and local honey. Villages along the routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5050081" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaviciosa&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5554225" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soto de Agues Village&lt;/a&gt;, are good places to look for refreshments and local eateries.
tips: In Plaza Manuel Uría, we find the church of San Bartolomé, an emblematic place in Nava. On August 24, the festival of its patron Saint Bartholomew is celebrated – a good opportunity to come and enjoy a weekend of festivities in a good atmosphere.

Fantastic museum where you can learn about the history and culture of Asturian cider. There are some beautiful old presses and you also learn the correct technique for pouring cider. The guides are fun and very knowledgable. You can also try Asturian cider from the barrel. Check the opening hours because they close during the winter.

The La Viona tunnel was part of a project to put a railway line between Sotrondrio and El Musel in Gijón. Although several tunnels were made, this being the largest, the project never saw the light of day.

The medieval bridge of Recuna is one of the medieval jewels of the Siero council. It gave access to the malataria, an important hospital institution that, in addition to lepers, was able to care for other patients and walkers. In addition, it is located on the route of one of the six main royal roads: the Camino Real from Villaviciosa to Pola de Siero within the Camino de Santiago.

Rolling through beautiful Asturia we find ourselves in La Pola Siero, where we can visit the church of San Pedro, the Valdepino palace, the Mendoza palace and the covered square. If we feel like taking a break, we can stop for a glass of cider, since La Pola Siero has a long tradition in cider production.

The Nora River Path is a tourist and recreational route that follows the course of the Nora River. It is a well marked and conditioned path that offers panoramic views of the river, the meadows and the mountains in the background. Along the path, you can find numerous places of interest and there are also recreational areas with tables, benches and picnic areas to rest and enjoy the scenery.

In the center of Nava, at the crossroads from which the Bimenes road starts, there is a beautiful Indian house known as "Villa Maximina". Construction of mountain architecture, it has a quadrangular floor plan with a tower to the right of the main façade and a terrace on another of its façades. The ceramic decoration stands out.

waypoints.name: Covered Square of Pola de Siero
